data.title: U.S. President Trump Announces Potential Actions Against Iran
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has announced that all bridges in Iran are set to be destroyed by midnight on Tuesday. According to Odaily, he also stated that all power plants in the country will cease operations before midnight. If an agreement with Iran is not reached, these power plants will be dismantled. Trump emphasized that he hopes to avoid such actions.

data.title: Trump Criticizes NATO and Allies Over Iran Conflict Assistance
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed dissatisfaction with South Korea, Australia, Japan, and NATO regarding their lack of support in the ongoing conflict with Iran. According to NS3.AI, Trump labeled NATO as a "paper tiger," criticizing the alliance for its hesitance to provide assistance.

data.title: JPMorgan Maintains Sell Rating on Tesla with $145 Price Target
data.description: JPMorgan has reaffirmed its sell rating on Tesla, setting a price target of $145, which suggests a potential downside of approximately 60% from current levels. According to NS3.AI, Tesla's reported deliveries for the first quarter of 2026 amounted to 358,023 vehicles, falling short of Wall Street's expectations.

data.title: IMF Chief: War Impacts Economic Growth Projections
data.description: IMF Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva stated that without the ongoing war, the IMF had initially planned to slightly raise its 2026 economic growth forecast of 3.3%. According to Jin10, Georgieva emphasized that current circumstances indicate a trend towards higher prices and slower growth.

data.title: US President Trump: I would rather the US collect tolls in the Strait of Hormuz than let Iran do it.
data.description: US President Trump: I'd rather the US collect tolls in the Strait of Hormuz than let Iran do it. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. President Trump Prefers U.S. to Collect Fees in Hormuz Strait
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump expressed his preference for the United States to collect transit fees in the Strait of Hormuz rather than allowing Iran to do so. According to Odaily, Trump's statement highlights his stance on maintaining U.S. influence and control in the strategic waterway.

data.title: US President Trump: All power plants will be dismantled if no deal is reached with Iran.
data.description: US President Trump: All bridges in Iran will be destroyed by midnight Tuesday; all power plants will be shut down by midnight. If no agreement is reached with Iran, all power plants will be dismantled. We don't want that to happen. (Jinshi)

data.title: Polymarket Traders Face Losses as Study Reveals 84.1% Are Losing Money
data.description: Independent on-chain analyst Andrey Sergeenkov has conducted a study revealing that a significant portion of Polymarket traders are experiencing financial losses. According to NS3.AI, the analysis examined 2.5 million Polygon wallet addresses, uncovering that only 840 addresses have managed to earn $100,000 through trading on the platform. This finding highlights the challenges faced by traders in navigating the complexities of the market.

data.title: OpenServ Introduces SERV Nano with Promises of Enhanced Efficiency
data.description: OpenServ has unveiled its latest AI infrastructure platform, SERV Nano, highlighting its potential advantages over models associated with OpenAI. According to NS3.AI, the company claims that SERV Nano offers a 20-fold reduction in costs and a threefold increase in speed for certain tasks. The platform integrates Base and Solana rails to facilitate token launch and monetization. However, independent public verification of benchmark methods and specific deployments is currently limited.

data.title: Alphabet Stock Faces Potential Challenges Amid CPI and Oil Price Concerns
data.description: Alphabet's stock outlook this week is influenced by the upcoming U.S. Consumer Price Index (CPI) release on April 10 and Brent crude oil prices remaining above $109. According to NS3.AI, the stock's $290 support level may be tested if the CPI exceeds 2.4%. Additionally, oil prices above $110 could disrupt the recent stock rebound. Investors are also closely monitoring volume trends ahead of Alphabet's Q1 earnings call scheduled for April 23.

data.title: Senate Banking Committee to Address Digital Asset Market Structure Bill in April
data.description: Sen. Bill Hagerty anticipates that the Senate Banking Committee will consider a digital asset market structure bill during the upcoming work period, aiming to advance it out of committee by April. According to NS3.AI, the bill requires further action from both the banking and agriculture committees. Progress has been hindered by disagreements over tokenized equities, ethical concerns, and stablecoin yield.

data.title: Brazil Faces Tight Labor Market and Uncontrolled Inflation Expectations
data.description: Brazilian officials have expressed concerns over the country's current economic conditions, highlighting a very tight labor market. According to Jin10, inflation expectations remain in an uncontrolled state, posing significant challenges for economic stability. The situation underscores the need for strategic measures to address these pressing issues.

data.title: Home Loan Approval Criteria: Income and Debt Considerations
data.description: Securing a $1 million home loan typically requires an annual salary of approximately $175,000. According to NS3.AI, banks also assess credit scores and total debt levels during the approval process. If debt exceeds 36% of income, it may hinder loan approval.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Meta Platforms to Release Next-Generation AI Model
data.description: Meta Platforms is set to release the open-source version of its next-generation AI model. According to Jin10, this move is part of Meta's ongoing efforts to advance artificial intelligence technology and make it more accessible to developers and researchers. The release is expected to provide new opportunities for innovation and collaboration within the AI community.

data.title: Bitcoin Surpasses $69,000 Amid Strait of Hormuz Tensions
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has set a deadline of Tuesday at 8:00 pm ET concerning the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. According to NS3.AI, this geopolitical development has drawn attention to Bitcoin's potential to reach $75,000. Bitcoin recently climbed above $69,000, marking its highest level in over ten days.

data.title: Bernstein Assigns Outperform Rating to Figure Technology with $67 Price Target
data.description: Bernstein has assigned an Outperform rating to Figure Technology, setting a price target of $67, which is nearly double its recent trading level of approximately $32. According to NS3.AI, this rating comes after Figure Technology reported $1.2 billion in loan originations for March and a total volume of $2.9 billion for the first quarter. The company has been expanding its tokenized credit marketplace, contributing to its strong performance.

data.title: Polymarket to Launch New Stablecoin for Enhanced Platform Efficiency
data.description: Polymarket has announced plans to introduce Polymarket USD, a new collateral stablecoin, as it transitions from its existing bridged USDC setup. According to NS3.AI, this upgrade aims to enhance security, improve trade execution speed, and reduce gas fees. The rollout will occur gradually over the coming weeks, marking a significant shift in the platform's operational framework.

data.title: Chaos Labs Ends Risk Management Partnership with Aave
data.description: Chaos Labs has announced the termination of its risk management partnership with Aave, citing concerns over budget allocation and staffing challenges. According to NS3.AI, Chaos Labs highlighted that Aave allocates only approximately 2% of its budget to risk management, which has raised concerns about the sustainability of their collaboration. Furthermore, the upcoming Aave V4 will necessitate a complete overhaul of its risk infrastructure, a task deemed unsustainable due to staffing limitations and key personnel departures. Chaos Labs emphasized the need for a robust risk management framework to ensure the protocol's long-term viability.

data.title: Figure Technology Solutions Sees Potential Growth Amid Lending Surge
data.description:
Figure Technology Solutions, a blockchain-based lending platform that went public last year, is considered potentially undervalued as its loan originations increase and its tokenized credit marketplace expands. According to Cointelegraph, Bernstein analysts have given Figure an “Outperform” rating with a $67 price target, nearly double its recent trading level of around $32.

The optimistic outlook follows a significant rise in lending activity. In March, Figure originated $1.2 billion in loans, a 33% increase from the previous month, marking the first time monthly volumes surpassed $1 billion. The company primarily focuses on home equity lines of credit (HELOCs), which allow homeowners to borrow against their property equity, usually at lower interest rates than unsecured loans. Utilizing the Provence blockchain, Figure claims to enhance efficiency in the loan process, reducing costs by 117 basis points per loan. First-quarter originations reached $2.9 billion, more than doubling from the previous year and defying the typical seasonal slowdown in HELOC demand. The company is now on track for approximately $12 billion in annualized loan volume.

Figure’s growth is attributed to rising consumer loan demand, an expanding partner network, and the ongoing rollout of its blockchain-based credit infrastructure, including its YLDS stablecoin. Despite a strong start to the year and a positive fourth quarter with increased earnings and revenue, Figure's profits fell short of expectations. The company's stock has struggled, declining over 20% this year amid broader volatility in digital asset-linked stocks and sector-specific challenges. Following its high-profile Nasdaq debut last September, which valued the company at nearly $800 million, the stock has faced difficulties regaining momentum.

Bernstein's analysis values Figure at roughly 25 times its projected 2027 EBITDA, indicating the stock trades at a multiple of its expected earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization. This valuation surpasses existing digital asset companies, reflecting Figure’s potential as both a tokenization platform and a profitable lending business. However, risks persist. HELOC demand can be sensitive to mortgage refinancing trends, and the broader private credit market, a crucial component of Figure’s growth strategy, has shown signs of increasing pressure.

data.title: Polymarket Plans Major Infrastructure Upgrade to Enhance Platform
data.description:
Polymarket, an on-chain prediction market platform, has announced a significant infrastructure upgrade set to occur within the next 2-3 weeks. According to ChainCatcher, this upgrade will include a comprehensive overhaul of the trading engine and the introduction of a native stablecoin, Polymarket USD.

The upgrade will feature the new CTF Exchange V2 smart contract system, which aims to optimize matching logic and order structure, improve matching efficiency, and reduce gas costs. Additionally, the platform will enhance its central limit order book (CLOB) by adopting a hybrid model of off-chain order matching and on-chain settlement.

On the asset side, Polymarket will introduce Polymarket USD, a native stablecoin pegged 1:1 to USDC, to replace the previous cross-chain version USDC.e on Polygon. This change is intended to improve asset security and consistency. The platform will also support the EIP-1271 standard, allowing multi-signature wallets, such as Safe, to participate directly in trading, thereby expanding use cases for institutional and advanced users.

Polymarket has stated that existing order books will be cleared during the upgrade, with specific maintenance times to be announced a week in advance. This upgrade comes amid increasing competition in the prediction market sector, as the platform seeks to enhance performance and user experience.
